Fall.notes: Full-semester courses typically run 16 weeks. 8-week classes may start/finish mid-semester.

Winter.notes: Full-semester courses typically run 16 weeks. 8-week classes may start/finish mid-semester.

Spring.notes: Shorter session; exact dates vary year-to-year. Consult registrar for calendar.

Summer.notes: Multiple mini-sessions / blocks; exact start/end dates vary.

religionRequirement.description: BYU requires religion credits for all students. Religion credits are tracked and evaluated separately from general electives and major/minor requirements.
commonCategories: Teachings & Doctrine of the Book of Mormon

rules.counting: Religion credits count toward overall earned credits but are also validated against religion-specific requirement blocks. Agent should present both earned-credit effect and religion-block fulfillment separately.
rules.frequency: Some programs may require a number of religion credits by graduation; others may require a minimum per time period—consult program metadata where available.

creditNoCredit.interpretation: P/CR counts as credit earned but typically does not carry grade points. Behavior may vary by program.

gradePointRules.note: Use registrar or program mapping for exact GPA calculations. Pass/CR values should not be included in GPA by default unless program-specific rules override.

credits.notes: Activity and wellness classes often have fractional credit amounts (0.25 or 0.5).

courseIdentifiers.subjectCode: String (e.g., MATH, REL A, IS, ME EN)

withdrawn.notes: W appears on transcript but does not count as earned credit.

repeats.policy_default: Both attempts appear on transcript; programmatic replacement or highest-grade rules must be supplied by program metadata.
repeats.agentBehavior: When evaluating degree progress, do not implicitly replace grades — consult program rule or registrar preference field.

transferCredits.notes: Transfer credits may have different subject mappings and may need articulation data. Agent must call an articulation mapping service or use provided transfer mappings.

eachItem.requirementType: nullable string (e.g., allOf, anyOf) -- may be missing

fulfillsRequirementsField.notes: A single course may match multiple program requirements; preserve all matched items and timestamps.

matchingRules.subjectNumberMatch: primary rule: subject code + course number
matchingRules.creditThreshold: match only if credits >= requirement's minimum unless explicitly allowed
matchingRules.optionBuckets: support rules like 'Complete N of M' and 'Complete X hours' — keep requirementId and requirementDescription intact for downstream logic

edgeCases: 8-week classes overlap with full-semester classes (start/end mid-semester); careful with per-term progress metrics.
Activity classes (0.25 or 0.5 credits) and ensemble/music participation credits (1 credit or less).
Courses can fulfill multiple distinct requirement blocks simultaneously (e.g., gen-ed + major elective).
Religion hours are required for ALL students and should be surfaced prominently; they may also count toward total credits.
Pass/CR grading may affect prerequisites or program eligibility differently — check program rules.
Course numbers with suffixes (e.g., 311R) indicate special types; treat suffix as part of the number.

programmaticHints.timestamps: Prefer registrar-sourced ISO8601 timestamps (e.g., matchedAt) for ordering and staleness checks.
programmaticHints.provenance: Keep origin (parsed/manual/registrar) and surface origin when recommending actions.
programmaticHints.freshness: Store lastUpdated on config and prefer per-program lastUpdated metadata for program-specific rules.
programmaticHints.validation: Agent should validate each course object against sampleCourse.schema before using it for degree checks.

schema.subject: String
schema.title: String
schema.fulfillsRequirements: Array of requirement-match objects (may be empty)

fulfillsRequirements.requirementDescription: The Individual and Society - American Heritage

jsonSchemaNotes.purpose: Not a full JSON Schema document, but a compact description of expected types/fields. For strict validation, produce a formal JSON Schema (I can generate if you want).
